parentQuestion: A new cancer drug cannot enter tumor cells because it is large and highly charge...

scaffolds.q: Can large, highly charged molecules easily cross the lipid bilayer?
scaffolds.explain: No! Large, charged molecules are blocked by the hydrophobic lipid bilayer. They need protein channels, carriers, or bulk transport mechanisms (endocytosis) to enter cells. The membrane is a selective fortress.

scaffolds.q: What is the general term for a cell engulfing material by wrapping it in membrane?
scaffolds.explain: Endocytosis ('endo' = into, 'cyto' = cell) — the cell wraps material in membrane and brings it inside as a vesicle. Exocytosis is the reverse (releasing material). Osmosis is water movement. Diffusion is passive spreading. Etymology guides you to the right answer.
